major_expressions: fabric / freedom / rest / rock / basis / gates / hell / prevail
faces / fall / victims / ravages / walls / erected / defense / trodden
forest / oaks / hurricane / trunk / verdure / foliage / murmur / breezes / limbs / storms / sink
living / history / family / history / bearing / testimonies / history / read
pillars / temple / liberty / crumbled / temple / fall / supply / pillars / hewn / quarry / reason
uprear / edifice / transmit / unprofaned / undecayed / untorn
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Makes national survival feel like the preservation of a living body rather than an abstract institutional choice.
rival_readings: A nation is not a biological organism, but it is construed as capable of living.
A polity is not a biological organism, but its political existence is construed as bodily life or death.
A social phenomenon is treated as a creature with an origin.
Abstract ambition is repeatedly construed through embodied motion, animal rank, appetite, and fire.
Abstract civic faculties and memorial expectation are treated through bodily and material processes.
Abstract danger or political actors are represented as emerging upward like growth.
Abstract destiny and ambition are represented as attached to an undertaking.
Abstract freedom is construed as a physical structure with a foundation and biblical fortification imagery.
Abstract passions and memories are treated as bodies, agents, or lights.
Ambition and fame are not literal game or crops, but are framed through hunting and agriculture.
An overseas army is personified as a giant capable of bodily attack.
Government is abstract-institutional but construed as an entity that can be bane or be annihilated.
Historical memory is embodied in persons and wounds as if it were a living readable document.
Human generations and memory are explicitly construed as trees weathered by storms.
Institutional maintenance is framed through structural support and material decay.
Passions and judgment are abstract faculties but are treated as agents with temperaments.
Political destruction is framed through authorship and completion.
Political institutions are explicitly construed as a built edifice.
Political liberty and rational support are explicitly construed as architectural materials.
Political work is construed as construction or demolition of a building.
Political-legal harm is represented through bodily orientation, falling, and ravaging.
Public affairs and attention are construed through combat and physical absorption.
The contextual meaning concerns burial/death rather than ordinary bodily rest after exertion.
The expression frames exploitation through bodily extraction and biblical labor idiom.
The nation is not literally born, yet its founding is framed through birth-generation language.
The nation is treated as an injured body capable of wounds and care.
The sentence explicitly compares corpses on trees with moss or drapery.
The world is treated as a remembering person.
War is not literally a whip, but it is explicitly called a scourge.
negative_cases: The current reviewed sample does not yet show a fully medicalized disease or purification logic.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports the body-politic corollary more directly than the sacrificial law itself.

cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Frames the civic relationship as a binding oath, converting political affiliation into solemn obligation.
political_actions_authorized: enduring war costs to honor the founding compact
rival_readings: Legal-political reverence is explicitly understood through religious ritual vocabulary.
The constitutional Union is abstract-political but construed as a separable material entity.
negative_cases: Covenant language in Lincoln is often implicit; direct invocations of oath are concentrated in the constitutional oath of office rather than the full corpus.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports the obligatory-frame dimension: citizens are bound by covenant to preserve the sacred object regardless of personal cost.

cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Makes the republic's survival feel like an empirical test of democratic self-governance before the world.
political_actions_authorized: completing the war to validate the republican proposition
rival_readings: A political order is not a laboratory object, but its success is framed as a trial or proof.
negative_cases: The experiment frame appears most clearly at Gettysburg; it is less developed in the Second Inaugural, which shifts to providential framing.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Raises the stakes of sacrifice by making soldier death proof that the democratic proposition is viable rather than merely honorable.

major_expressions: fortress / strength / artillery / time / leveling / walls
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Constructs political obligation as inherited from founding fathers, making the republic a bequest that the current generation must not squander.
rival_readings: Collective historical existence is explicitly framed as appearing on a stage.
Events under the sun are not literally a ledger, but the sentence frames history through accounting.
Memory is not a fortification, and time does not literally fire artillery.
Reputation and memory are treated through bodily life/death or physical descent.
negative_cases: The fathers/inheritance frame in Lincoln typically constructs an all-male genealogy that excludes women and the enslaved from founding agency.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports the sacred-object and obligatory-frame dimensions: the republic is a patrimony whose preservation obligates the heirs.

major_expressions: Woe / offenses / offenses / woe / offense / offenses / providence / gives / woe / offense
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Places slavery, bloodshed, guilt, and reconciliation under divine judgment rather than partisan triumph.
political_actions_authorized: accepting war suffering as moral judgment
rival_readings: Biblical usage may be conventional theological idiom rather than a separable metaphor.
The living are not physically set apart like an object or place; they are morally committed to a public task.
negative_cases: Providence language can be doctrinal quotation or rhetorical convention; it should not be used as evidence of private belief without corroboration.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports historical-sacral framing and guilt distribution, while limiting enemy-destruction claims through reconciliation.

cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Marks a structural gap: the metaphorical systems reviewed raise the question of where Black political and military agency is represented or suppressed.
moral_emotions_activated: critical attention to what is missing
awareness of whose sacrifice is named versus whose is erased
political_actions_authorized: recognition of Black political agency as analytically required by the evidence
rival_readings: Wealth is not literally submerged; it is imagined as disappearing downward or being consumed.
negative_cases: This cluster is currently under-evidenced in the MIPVU review; it marks a review question rather than a confirmed finding.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports the absence-and-agency dimension: the Koenigsbergian framework requires attention to whose agency is suppressed by the dominant metaphor systems.

cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Converts soldier death into an offering that obligates the living.
rival_readings: Human death or removal is framed through theatrical stage imagery.
Revolutionary inheritance and legal liberty are treated as bodily/material things that can be spilled, trampled, or torn.
The killing is historical violence, not a ritual offering, but is named through sacrificial vocabulary.
negative_cases: The current evidence sacralizes Union death but does not make killing an end in itself.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Directly supports the sacrificial-body dimension, especially in Gettysburg's battlefield setting.
